items.question: What stacks does project-to-any support?
items.answer: Currently: Angular (17+), Express + Node, and the MEAN stack (MongoDB, Express, Angular, Node). React, Next.js, Ionic, WordPress, and static HTML are planned.

items.question: Do I need to know CSS to use SuperCSS?
items.answer: No. SuperCSS works through data attributes on the html element. You switch themes, palettes, and modes with one attribute change — no CSS writing required. If you want to customize, the token system is designed to be easy to override.

items.question: Is the mock data compatible with real databases?
items.answer: Yes. The mock data is designed to match real database schemas. The same sample records exist across SQL, MongoDB, Firestore, Redis, and JSON formats with identical IDs. Swap the data source and your UI works unchanged.

items.question: How does the view library work with Angular?
items.answer: Each page definition in the view library maps to an Angular standalone component. Run the compiler with --target angular and it generates the component files, wires the router, and links the content. The sections map to sub-components.

items.question: Can I use project-to-any for client work?
items.answer: Yes. The team license covers unlimited client projects. Each project runs independently and owns its own manifest. Your client never needs to know how it was built.

items.question: What happens if I need help?
items.answer: We offer community support via Discord for all users, email support on the Pro plan, and dedicated Slack support on the Team license. Response times are under 4 hours during business hours.

items.question: Is there a free tier?
items.answer: Yes. The starter tier is free forever and includes the view library, SuperCSS base, and mock data layer. Paid plans unlock the compiler targets, premium themes, and priority support.

items.question: Can I self-host?
items.answer: Yes. Every template generates fully portable output with no runtime dependency on Acme Studio. You can host anywhere — Vercel, Railway, Render, your own VPS, or a CDN.
